topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink on the paper felt like a brand against my chest. A C-minus. It was a catastrophic failure, a direct contradiction to the hours I’d poured into that history essay. I stared at the grade, my hands trembling slightly as I traced the poorly written critique. A hollow ache settled deep in my stomach, a heavy, suffocating weight. I remembered the late nights fueled by lukewarm coffee, the meticulous research, the sheer confidence I’d felt before submitting it. Now, all that effort seemed to have dissolved into this disappointing mark. I picked up my pen, not to correct the work, but just to turn the page, the movement slow and deliberate, as if wading through thick mud.

[story 2]

“Another disaster,” I muttered under my breath, the sound barely escaping my lips. The notification popped up on my screen, stark and unforgiving. A score far below the B I’d been anticipating. I slumped back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning in protest. My shoulders dropped, pulling inward as if trying to disappear into the fabric of the seat. I squeezed my eyes shut, replaying the moments of carelessness—the rushed citations, the assumption that I understood the nuance—and a hot flush crept up my neck. I needed to fix this, to somehow rewind the clock, but the reality was that the assignment was done, and the outcome was fixed. I rubbed my temples, the pressure behind my eyes intensifying with a profound sense of failure.

[story 3]

The fluorescent lights of the library seemed to hum with judgment. I unfolded the graded paper, and the discrepancy between my expectations and the actual result hit me like a physical blow. My breath hitched, shallow and quick. I looked around the silent study area, suddenly hyper-aware of every passing student, every focused brow. A tightness constricted my throat. I picked up a pencil, its graphite cool against my fingertips, and began to doodle meaningless spirals on the margin of the page, my focus utterly shattered. Every muscle in my jaw felt clenched, a physical manifestation of the internal turmoil churning within me. I just wanted the weight of that disappointing number to vanish.
